businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD shifted to a $99/month subscription model. Key catalysts in April 2026: Cybercab production start and Roadster reveal.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Extreme premium persists even after -27% from 52-week high

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Over 4x typical growth stock multiples of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $94.8B, barely grew. 2026E consensus is +15% to $108.9B

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: YTD -20%. Still larger than Toyota, VW, GM, and Ford combined, but shrinking fast

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Extreme selling pressure but technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: EV market down 28% yet Tesla market share rises above 50% (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Despite a 28% plunge in the overall EV market, Tesla expanded its share above 50%. Brand power and charging infrastructure become stronger competitive advantages during downturns

growthDrivers.title: SpaceX IPO rumors as valuation catalyst (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: SpaceX IPO preparation rumors spread, potentially serving as an indirect catalyst for Tesla investors and triggering a re-rating of the Musk ecosystem

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video revealed, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla showed Optimus3 reduction gears and precision hand design. Targeting 1M production rate by end of 2026. Trefis values the robot business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster late April reveal
growthDrivers.description: Both the steer-free Cybercab robotaxi and next-gen Roadster are scheduled for April, creating a dual catalyst within weeks

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed $600 target calling Terafab a game changer. AI5 chip small-batch production starts late 2026, full production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD investigation escalation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its FSD probe over performance issues in fog and dust. Trefis warns this could break the '$1T AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market share 50%+, FSD 8.4B miles of driving data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab for in-house AI chips

moats.description: 8.4B miles of real-world FSD driving data. Scale that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantages and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China #1, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price advantage rapidly closing the gap

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service in SF and Las Vegas. Pilot operations in Austin and Miami

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in commercial autonomous ride-hailing with 200K+ paid trips per week

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: 'No Kings Day 3.0' protests continue worldwide, 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations confirmed
recentNews.significance: Post-protest coverage intensifies. The Guardian covered 'next steps after the march.' Reuters reported small-town participation surged 40% from the first mobilization. Anti-Tesla sentiment spreading beyond major cities

recentNews.headline: Millions join 'No Kings' protests worldwide, Tesla targeted at 200+ locations
recentNews.significance: Thousands of protests with millions of participants. Musk's DOGE political activities made Tesla a direct protest target with 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations

recentNews.headline: Musk seeks jury bias probe in Twitter trial
recentNews.significance: Musk's legal team claims jurors mocked him with $4.20 verdict, requesting bias investigation. Legal risks persist

recentNews.headline: Tesla market share rises above 50% amid 28% EV market plunge
recentNews.significance: Tesla expanded share even as the broader EV market collapsed. Proof that the strong get stronger in downturns

recentNews.headline: HSBC slashes Tesla target to $119 from $133, sees 67% further downside
recentNews.significance: HSBC further cut its target from $133 to $119 with Reduce rating. Contrasts sharply with Stifel's $508 target. Extreme analyst divergence

analystOpinions.notableComment: HSBC cut from $133 to $119, the new Street low. Stifel at $508 and Wedbush at $600 at the other extreme. Morgan Stanley at $415. Extreme polarization continues

risks.title: 'No Kings' protests spreading to small towns
risks.description: Reuters reports small-town protest participation surged 40% from the first mobilization. European registrations already -17%. Brand damage becoming structural as boycotts spread nationwide

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages
risks.description: Trading below SMA50 ($387) and SMA200 ($400). RSI at 33 near oversold but no trend reversal signals. Five consecutive trading days of decline

risks.title: Extreme valuation risk
risks.description: TTM P/E of 335x is 14.5x the S&P 500 average of 23x. HSBC targets $119, projecting an additional 67% decline

risks.title: HSBC cuts target to $119
risks.description: HSBC further lowered its target from $133 to $119, citing FSD commercialization uncertainty and weakening core auto business

risks.title: Q1 delivery concerns intensifying
risks.description: Consensus at 365,645 units, -13% vs Q4. European boycotts and protest expansion could push actual deliveries below consensus

risks.title: FSD regulatory and safety risks
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalated. California robotaxi application denied. Trefis warns the '$1T AI thesis could collapse'

buyReasons.title: Market share expansion during EV downturn proves competitive strength
buyReasons.rationale: Tesla grew share above 50% even as the EV market crashed 28%. The strong get stronger in downturns

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration (Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval, and Q1 earnings (4/21) all within 3 weeks

buyReasons.title: RSI 33, entering technical oversold territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching the 30 oversold threshold. 27% below 52-week high. Increasing probability of technical bounce

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus robot as long-term re-rating catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production start could justify Tesla's long-term valuation premium

overallOpinion: 'No Kings' protests are spreading to small towns. Reuters reports small-town participation surged 40% from the first mobilization. The anti-Tesla movement is structurally damaging the brand beyond major cities

HSBC cut its target from $133 to $119, projecting a 67% decline. Stifel sits at $508. A 5x gap between the most bullish and bearish targets on the same company tells you everything about Tesla's polarization
April catalysts are packed within 3 weeks: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, 4/21 earnings. EV market share above 50% despite a 28% market crash proves fundamental strength
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below SMA50 ($387) and SMA200 ($400). Whether protest expansion hits Q1 deliveries is April's biggest variable. Hold, wait for 4/21 earnings

investmentVerdict.summary: RSI 33 oversold and April catalyst concentration support a bounce case, but protests spreading to small towns and HSBC's further cut to $119 signal strengthening bearish sentiment. European registrations already -17%. Q1 deliveries may face additional headwinds. Waiting for 4/21 earnings is the rational approach

threads.hook: TSLA: Protests spread to small towns + HSBC cuts to $119. April decides everything
threads.text:
$TSLA $361.83, market cap $1.16T, YTD -20%

No Kings protests are spreading to small towns. Reuters says participation surged 40% from the first mobilization. HSBC cut its target from $133 to $119.

Stifel at $508 vs HSBC at $119. A 5x gap that captures Tesla's polarization perfectly.

April: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, 4/21 earnings. EV market share 50%+ in a 28% crash is proof of strength.

RSI 33 oversold. Hold. 4/21 earnings will set the direction.

witty.hook: Stifel says $508. HSBC says $119. Same company, right?
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83

Stifel: $508 (+40%)
HSBC: $119 (-67%)

Same company. 5x target gap. Meanwhile, protests just hit small towns and Musk is arguing about jury bias.

1/4
thread:
EV market crashes 28%, Tesla grabs 50%+ share.
That's dominance.

But when there's a picket line at the dealership,
market share becomes theoretical

2/4

Reuters: small-town protest turnout up 40%.
Boycotts are becoming a cultural movement.

This is the risk that doesn't show on charts

3/4

RSI 33 oversold. April catalysts stacked.

Somewhere between $119 and $508 lies the truth.
4/21 will tell. Hold

4/4

But the company itself isn't broken

witty.hook: Stifel $508, HSBC $119. Tesla is a 5x debate
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83, YTD -20%

Stifel: $508. HSBC: $119. Same company. That 5x gap is Tesla's identity crisis in a number.

Protests hitting small towns now. Reuters: +40%. When boycotts become culture, market share gets theoretical.

But April has Cybercab, Roadster, and earnings. Fear and hope collide in the same month.

RSI 33. 4/21 will tell. Hold.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, protests spreading and HSBC downgrades. Really tough stretch
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83, down 27% from highs.

Protests in small towns, HSBC at $119. Every headline hurts when you're holding Tesla. It's a genuinely difficult time.

But 50%+ EV market share in a 28% crash. April catalysts packed. Stifel and HSBC 5x apart shows even pros can't agree.

RSI 33, selling pressure near exhaustion. Wait for 4/21 earnings. Patience is a strategy.
